steps.title: Backsolving - testing choice B
steps.content: Choice B (‘The crew has been unloading about 4.5 crates per hour.’) claims the crew unloads about 4.5 crates per hour. In the linear model $$y = 126 - 28x$$, the slope $$-28$$ indicates the number of crates is decreasing at 28 crates per hour. Since $4.5 \ne 28$, choice B (‘The crew has been unloading about 4.5 crates per hour.’) does not describe the x-intercept.

steps.title: Backsolving - testing choice C
steps.content: Choice C (‘The crew has been unloading about 28 crates per hour.’) states the crew unloads about 28 crates per hour. While the slope magnitude is 28 crates/hour, the x-intercept represents a time, not a rate. Therefore choice C (‘The crew has been unloading about 28 crates per hour.’) does not interpret the x-intercept.

steps.title: Backsolving - testing choice A
steps.content: To find the x-intercept set $$y = 0$$: $$0 = 126 - 28x$$ $$\to$$ $$28x = 126$$ $$\to$$ $$x = \frac{126}{28} = 4.5$$. This means after 4.5 hours no crates remain, i.e., the crew has finished unloading all the crates. That confirms A is correct.
